result:
**Goal: Add a "Genesis Protocol" mode — an autonomous open-ended evolution engine that seeds random rule combinations, runs them forward, scores them for emergent complexity using information-theoretic metrics, and curates a ranked gallery of the most interesting discovered universes.**

This is the natural next step in the project's recent trajectory (butterfly effect → computation detector → symbiosis → recursive sim). The existing infrastructure already has rule mutation, auto-discovery, and computation detection — Genesis Protocol ties them together into a single autonomous loop: **generate → simulate → measure → rank → display**. The user launches it and watches as the system independently discovers rule-spaces that produce gliders, oscillators, self-replicating structures, or phase transitions, building a persistent hall-of-fame of the most complex emergent universes it finds. It turns the simulator from a tool you operate into one that explores on its own.

Want me to build it?
